name: lint-fixer
description: Use this skill to run npm run lint and fix linting issues. Triggers on fixing lint errors after code changes or validating code against project style guidelines.
capabilities: Run the npm run lint workflow in projects
Apply Biome and oxlint auto fixes for style issues
Run TypeScript type checking with tsgo
Run secretlint for secret and credential detection
Guide manual fixes for remaining lint errors
author: yamadashy
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill with no executable code. Contains only markdown documentation and JSON metadata files. The static findings are all false positives triggered by tool names, license identifiers, and hash fields in documentation - not actual malicious code or command execution. This skill describes a legitimate lint-fixing workflow.
valueStatement: Lint failures slow down code reviews and block releases. This skill runs the project lint workflow and guides fixes while keeping code behavior unchanged.
userTitle: Fix lint issues with project lint workflow
limitations: Requires npm run lint to be configured in the project
Type errors from tsgo require manual fixes
Does not change code behavior to satisfy lint rules
Test execution depends on npm run test availability

useCases.title: Pre merge lint cleanup
useCases.description: Fix style and lint errors before opening a pull request.

useCases.title: Team lint consistency
useCases.description: Apply standard lint fixes after shared refactors across the codebase.

useCases.title: Reduce CI lint failures
useCases.description: Guide contributors to resolve lint errors that block the CI pipeline.

promptTemplates.title: Run lint and fix
promptTemplates.scenario: After code changes in a JS project
promptTemplates.prompt: Run npm run lint, list failures by tool, fix safe issues, and re run lint to confirm.

promptTemplates.title: Type error focus
promptTemplates.prompt: Run npm run lint, focus on tsgo errors, explain each fix, and keep behavior unchanged.

promptTemplates.title: Secretlint check
promptTemplates.prompt: Run npm run lint, review secretlint results, and remove any detected secrets safely.

promptTemplates.title: Strict lint pass
promptTemplates.prompt: Run npm run lint, apply Biome and oxlint auto fixes, then re run lint and summarize remaining issues.

bestPractices: Review auto fixes before committing changes to ensure they match intent
Re run npm run lint after each fix round to track progress
Run npm run test after lint passes to verify no breakage
antiPatterns: Changing runtime behavior just to satisfy lint rules
Ignoring secretlint findings without reviewing them
Skipping the final lint rerun before committing

faq.question: What projects are supported?
faq.answer: Projects that define npm run lint with Biome, oxlint, tsgo, and secretlint in package scripts.

faq.question: What are the known limits?
faq.answer: Type errors from tsgo need manual fixes and are not auto resolved by the skill.

faq.question: How do I integrate this in CI?
faq.answer: Use it before or after a CI lint step to guide fixes and verify the lint pass.

faq.question: Does it access my private data?
faq.answer: No. It only runs project lint commands that already exist in your repository.

faq.question: What if lint still fails after using this?
faq.answer: Check remaining errors, fix them manually, and re run npm run lint until it passes.

faq.question: How is this different from a code formatter?
faq.answer: It runs multiple lint and type checking tools, not just formatting rules.
